hub_subtitle: Medical English to welcome guests and present with confidence

lesson.menu_desc: Write a simple work email: subject, Dear, a short message, and Best regards; join ideas with and / but / because -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Writing a Short Email
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Graziele Dias | Aula 11 -- Writing a Short Email

listenings.text: Hello Graziele. I am reading you my email. Subject: Meeting Confirmation. Dear Graziele, thank you for your message. I confirm our meeting on Monday at three o'clock. I am available in the afternoon, but not in the morning. Please reply if you have a question. Best regards, Doctor Carter.

Hi Graziele, this is Ana. A short tip about emails. Every email has three parts. First, the greeting: Dear Doctor Carter. Second, the message: write it short and clear. Third, the closing: Best regards, and your name. Start with Dear, and finish with Best regards. It is easy. Good luck with your email!

extra_audio.text: Graziele writes the subject of her email. She writes the greeting: Dear Dr. Carter. She writes a short message to confirm the meeting. She finishes with Best regards. Then she sends the email.
